description: Quality-gate contract for /tl-telar:orchestrate. Phase 2 VALIDATE reads enforcement.* commands and runs each. Strict flags determine whether a non-zero exit blocks COMMIT.

autonomy.description: interactive (default) = the orchestrator may pause mid-cycle at checkpoint:true WUs and at self-reflect. unattended = exactly ONE human gate at plan-ready (Step 5): scope + approved UI ASCII drafts + all secrets/inputs are collected up front, then the WU cycle (Step 6) runs to PR-ready with ZERO pauses. UI/visual sign-off is hoisted into plan-readiness, never mid-cycle. Unattended never skips a decision — it makes it earlier. See agents/mobile-orchestrator.md -> 'Autonomy model'.

execution.description: Maximum Work Units the orchestrator runs concurrently (background subagents). A WU is dispatched only when its deps are COMPLETE and its file_scope is disjoint from every running WU. Default 3 (matches Anthropic 3-5 subagent guidance); raise only for many small, disjoint WUs — parallel agent runs cost ~15x chat tokens. See scripts/tl-telar-wu-scheduler.js.

enforcement.coverage_command: echo 'coverage not configured - run /tl-telar:setup-orchestration for framework-aware defaults' && exit 0
enforcement.description: Mechanical gates (coverage, file-scope) are always blocking. Mobile-extended gates (perf, size, a11y) are advisory by default; set *_strict: true to make them blocking. See rules/quality-gates.md for the tier doctrine.
